domain: Model Routing, Cascading & Orchestration for SLMs

scoring_notes.demand: 0-10: How many people want this? Based on search signals, community activity, research volume.
scoring_notes.competition_gap: 0-10: How underserved is this? 10 = no solution exists, 0 = fully solved by existing tools.
scoring_notes.feasibility: 0-10: How buildable is this as a startup/OSS project? Considers technical complexity, data requirements, go-to-market.

gaps.title: Edge-Native SLM Cascading Router
gaps.rationale: Demand: Massive research activity, Gartner forecast, Dell/AWS/telco use cases. Competition: Zero production tools — all cloud-first. Only vLLM mentions edge. Feasibility: Technically challenging (on-device runtime, heterogeneous hardware) but frameworks like Ollama/llama.cpp provide building blocks.

gaps.title: Multi-Step Agentic Workflow Router
gaps.rationale: Demand: Agentic AI is the hottest trend in 2026. DAAO, Helium, Anthropic guides all point here. Competition: Research frameworks exist (DAAO, Helium) but no production-ready tool. LLMRouter mentions agentic but early. Feasibility: High — can build on existing agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI) as plugins.

gaps.title: Confidence-Calibrated Cascading Framework
gaps.rationale: Demand: Dedicated 2026 survey, ICLR papers, 16x efficiency gains demonstrated. Competition: Only research implementations — no production tool. RouteLLM does binary routing, not calibrated multi-step cascading. Feasibility: Requires confidence calibration research implementation; moderate ML expertise needed.

gaps.title: Budget-Constrained Adaptive Routing Optimizer
gaps.rationale: Demand: Universal pain point — every team has a budget. Dedicated arXiv paper, multiple cost guides. Competition: Tools offer hard caps (Portkey, Helicone) but none optimize quality within budget dynamically. Feasibility: High — contextual bandit approach is well-understood; can integrate with existing gateways.

gaps.title: Domain-Specialized Routing (Code, Legal, Medical, etc.)
gaps.rationale: Demand: 45% of top law firms exploring domain models, IBM/ACM validating trend. Competition: No router offers domain-specific profiles. LiteLLM requires manual config. Feasibility: Needs domain-specific eval benchmarks and model profiling; doable but domain expertise required.

gaps.title: Open-Source Quality-Aware Router with Evaluation Loop
gaps.rationale: Demand: Massive OSS community interest (LLMRouter 1K+ stars, multiple HN Show HNs). Competition: Gap narrowed — LLMRouter is comprehensive, RouteLLM is quality-aware. The eval feedback loop remains missing. Feasibility: High — combine existing OSS routers with eval frameworks (DeepEval, LangSmith).

gaps.title: Compliance-Aware Routing (GDPR, HIPAA, Data Residency)
gaps.rationale: Demand: Strong in regulated industries (healthcare, finance, EU). Competition: Only TrueFoundry offers geo-aware routing. No tool combines compliance rules with quality optimization. Feasibility: Requires deep compliance knowledge and certification processes; longer GTM.

gaps.title: Hybrid Local/Cloud Router with Privacy Preservation
gaps.rationale: Demand: Privacy-sensitive industries need this. PII-aware routing tutorials and research papers confirm interest. Competition: PII detection exists (Requesty), PrivateLoRA is research. No integrated local-router-first product. Feasibility: Moderate — requires on-device runtime, adds complexity. Overlaps with GAP-001.

gaps.title: Real-Time Routing Benchmarks and Model Performance Tracker
gaps.rationale: Demand: Model regression is a real pain point. LiveBench/Artificial Analysis show generic demand. Competition: Generic benchmarks exist. LangSmith evaluates but does not auto-adjust routing. The closed loop is missing. Feasibility: Can build on existing eval infrastructure; requires continuous running costs.

gaps.title: Per-User Personalized Routing
gaps.rationale: Demand: PersonalizedRouter paper (TMLR), AWS guide, VC interest. Competition: LLMRouter already includes PersonalizedRouter — gap is shrinking. AWS provides guidance. Feasibility: Needs user interaction data collection; cold-start problem.

gaps.title: Fine-Tuned SLM Router Trainer (AutoML for Routing)
gaps.rationale: Demand: Teams want easy router training. Competition: NVIDIA Blueprint, LLMRouter pipeline, and RouteLLM all provide training tools now. The gap for true one-click AutoML is smaller. Feasibility: Data pipeline exists; UX layer is the main challenge.

gaps.title: Routing-as-SDK for Framework Integration
gaps.rationale: Demand: 14+ agent frameworks need routing. Competition: Google ADK, NVIDIA NeMo, and LiteLLM partially solve this. Feasibility: Straightforward engineering but requires maintaining integrations across fast-moving frameworks.

gaps.title: Multi-Modal Routing (Text + Image + Audio + Video)
gaps.rationale: Demand: Multimodal AI is growing but routing across modalities is nascent. Competition: LLMRouter added multimodal support. ICCV workshop paper exists. Feasibility: Requires benchmarks across modalities; evaluation is harder than text-only.

gaps.title: Cost-Quality Pareto Frontier Visualization and Exploration
gaps.rationale: Demand: Moderate — useful but not critical. Competition: Pareto frontier visualizers already exist (GitHub project, Artificial Analysis). Feasibility: Easy to build but hard to differentiate. Better as a feature than a product.

gaps.title: Streaming/Real-Time Routing with Mid-Response Escalation
gaps.rationale: Demand: Low explicit demand despite being technically interesting. Competition: Almost no one is doing this — high novelty. Feasibility: Very challenging technically (mid-stream switching, context handoff, latency management). Research-grade for now.
